title: SSL certificate error on macOS for update-skill
author: Vuk97

stage1.summary: Confirmed from code. The _download function in update_skill/cmd.py (line 29) uses urllib.request.urlopen directly with no SSL context customization. On macOS with Homebrew Python, the system SSL certificates are not available to Python by default -- users must run 'Install Certificates.command' or use certifi. This is a well-known macOS/Homebrew Python issue. The error is caught at line 159 (urllib.error.URLError) and printed as 'Download failed: ...' with no actionable guidance. The workaround (REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE env var) works because certifi is installed as a dependency.
conditions: Fix should try certifi first, fall back to default SSL context, and print a helpful message on SSL failure suggesting the certifi workaround or the Install Certificates.command
stage1.suggested_fix: In _download, create an ssl.SSLContext that loads certifi's CA bundle if available: try importing certifi, create ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where()), pass that context to urlopen. If certifi is not installed, fall back to default. On SSL failure, print a specific message: 'SSL certificate verification failed. Try: pip install certifi, or on macOS run /Applications/Python*/Install\ Certificates.command'. This is a 10-line fix in _download.
